title: ERD Node Selection with Transparent Non-Connected Nodes
story: As a DataTools Pro user exploring entity relationships, I want clicking on an ERD node to highlight it and its connected nodes while fading unrelated nodes to 80% transparency, so that I can visually trace relationship paths through a complex diagram.
description:
Implements a node selection highlighting system in the ERD diagram. When a user clicks on a node:

- The selected node and all directly connected nodes remain fully opaque
- All non-connected nodes and links fade to 80% transparency (opacity 0.2)
- Outbound links from the selected node are highlighted in green
- Inbound links to the selected node are highlighted in blue
- Multiple nodes can be selected simultaneously, and all their connections remain highlighted
- Clicking the diagram background resets all nodes and links to full opacity

This uses GoJS transaction-based updates with `nodeClicked` and `diagramClicked` handlers. A working CodePen example was provided as the reference implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a node highlights it and its directly connected nodes at full opacity
Non-connected nodes and links fade to 80% transparency (opacity 0.2)
Outbound links from the selected node are highlighted in green
Inbound links to the selected node are highlighted in blue
Multiple node selections accumulate — all selected nodes and their connections remain highlighted
Clicking the diagram background resets all nodes and links to full opacity
The highlight behavior uses GoJS transactions for clean state management
ux_changes: Clicking an ERD node highlights it and its connections while fading unrelated nodes to 80% transparency. Outbound links turn green, inbound links turn blue. Multi-select supported. Click background to reset.
implementation_notes: Implemented using GoJS `nodeClicked` and `diagramClicked` event handlers wrapped in transactions. A CodePen reference (https://codepen.io/datatoolspro/pen/LYKVOvR) was provided with the complete JS implementation including the highlight logic, opacity management, and multi-select support. The implementation iterates all nodes and links to set opacity based on connection status to selected nodes.
comment_summary: Developer picked up the task and implemented it on development, deploying quickly for review. The reference CodePen provided a complete working example that was adapted.
